---
title: "testingassignment4"
format: html
editor: visual
---


```{r}

#' Assignment 4
#' Simpson's Index (D)
#'
#' This function calculates Simpson's Index from a list of species names.
#' Formula used: 
#' D = sum(n * (n - 1)) / (N * (N-1))
#' where n is the number of individuals in one species, 
#' and N is the total number of individuals of all species.
#' 
#' @param species A vector of species names.
#' @return Simpson's index D.
#'
# function definition
simpsons_index = function(species) {

  # Count the total number of individuals
  N  = length(species) # length is a built-in function that counts the number of elements in a vector

  # Simpson's Index needs at least 2 individuals
  if (N < 2) {
    stop("You need at least two individuals!")
  }

  # Store species names and counts
  species_names = c() # empty vector to store unique species names
  species_counts = c() # empty vector to store counts of each species

  # Loop through all individuals
  for (i in 1:N) {

    current_species = species[i] # get the species name of the current individual 

    # Variable to count matches
    match_count = 0 

    # Check if species already exists in our list of unique species 
    if (length(species_names) > 0) { 

      for (j in 1:length(species_names)) { # Then we loop through the list of unique species names to see if we have a match

        if(current_species == species_names[j]) {
          species_counts[j] = species_counts[j] + 1 
          match_count = match_count + 1
        }

      }

    }

    # If species was not found, add it to the list of unique species and set count to 1 
    if (match_count == 0) {
      species_names = c(species_names, current_species) # add the new species name to the list of unique species names
      species_counts = c(species_counts, 1) # add a count of 1 for the new species to the list of counts 
    }

  }

  # Compute numerator of formula
  numerator = 0 

  for (i in 1:length(species_counts)) { # Loop through the list of counts for each species to compute the numerator of the formula 
    n = species_counts[i] # get the count for the current species 

    numerator = numerator + n * (n - 1) # add n * (n - 1) to the numerator for the current species 

  }

  # Commpute Simpson's Index
  result = numerator  / (N * (N - 1))

  return(result)

}

# --------------------------
# Test examples 
# --------------------------

# Example one: three species with the same number of individuals 
species_ex_1 = c("alligator", "panda", "penguin", "alligator", "panda", "penguin", "alligator", "panda", "penguin" )
D_ex_1 = simpsons_index(species_ex_1)
D_ex_1

# Example two: one species is more common than the others
species_ex_2 = c("panda", "panda", "panda", "panda", "panda", "penguin", "alligator")
D_ex_2 = simpsons_index(species_ex_2)
print(D_ex_2)

# Example three: all individuals are the same 
species_ex_3 = c("panda", "panda", "panda", "panda", "panda", "panda", "panda")
D_ex_3 = simpsons_index(species_ex_3)
print(D_ex_3)









```
